RaceName: Juddmonte 'Lead Artist' Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Dark Angel colt sent off at 4-1 on debut over 6f here (gd-fm) 22 days ago and found things happening a bit quickly before staying on very nicely into fifth, after meeting traffic problems; wasn't knocked about and looks almost certain to improve for this longer trip.

RaceName: Juddmonte 'Lead Artist' Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; 200,000gns foal; half-brother to 1m-1m2f winner Garden Route (RPR 93); dam South African 5f-6f 2yo winner (inc Group 1), half-sister to South African 1m Group 1 winner Silver Mountain; nicely bred and holds Group 1 entries, but looks down the stable's pecking order on rider arrangements.

RaceName: Juddmonte 'Lead Artist' Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit
Spotlight: Starspangledbanner colt; E150,000 yearling; dam French 1m 2yo winner (RPR 96), sister to 7f winner Borjina, half-sister to useful 1m4f/12.5f winner Plage De Havre, out of maiden half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 2 winner Rich Legacy; holds Group 1 entry; market can guide.

RaceName: Juddmonte 'Lead Artist' Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Frankel colt; brother to winners Homeless Songs (7f/1m inc 2yo/Irish 1,000 Guineas; RPR 122) and Spoken Truth (1m2f; 98), half-brother to winners Reve De Vol (7f 2yo/1m2f Listed; 106) and Time Tells All (1m2f; 85); dam 7f winner (inc Listed; 104); impeccably bred and holds multiple Group 1 entries; interesting debutant.

RaceName: Juddmonte 'Lead Artist' Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; dam Group-placed 8.4f winner (RPR 104), sister to winners Delphi (Australian 1m4f Group 2), Matchless (7f-1m4f Flat and 2m hurdle) and Last Galileo (1m 2yo), out of 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; preferred by Moore and yard has farmed this race in recent years.

RaceName: Juddmonte 'Lead Artist' Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; brother to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Beautify (RPR 107), half-brother to winners Library (7.5f 2yo/1m2f Listed; 109) and Vice President (1m2f; 93); dam 7f 2yo/1m4f Group 3 winner (105); lots to like on paper and Lordan rode The Lion In Winter when he won this maiden in 2023; can't discount.

RaceName: Betway Free Bet Club Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Cherry Knoll Farm, M Ward & D Skelton
Spotlight: Doubly penalised for winning a match at Huntingdon (2m4f145yds) and a weak race at Uttoxeter (2m; again on good), since when he's undergone wind surgery; hasn't been beating a lot but he's probably progressive.

RaceName: Betway Free Bet Club Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Progressive form over shorter, winning a maiden over an extended 2m at Kilbeggan (good to yielding); weakened over 2m4f next time and then ran poorly at 3m; strong form claims if he can prove himself over this far.

RaceName: Betway Free Bet Club Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bumper winner who made all in a Kelso novice hurdle (2m, good); came up shy under a penalty at Hexham but was clear of the rest in chasing home the odds-on favourite; steps up in distance this afternoon.

RaceName: Betway Free Bet Club Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-22 in points and now 0-18 under rules (0-14 over fences) after finishing a tailed-off runner-up in a novice hurdle at Newton Abbot.

RaceName: Betway Free Bet Club Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-11 on the Flat and has been contesting low-grade handicaps at about 2m; unlikely to be winning this on hurdle debut.

RaceName: Betway Free Bet Club Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Went close in two bumpers and was pulled up sharply on hurdle debut when jockey felt something was amiss; has since left David Pipe; bred to improve for this longer distance but he needs to.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Promising start in 7f maiden at Leopardstown in May, beaten 3.25l into fourth of nine despite racing a shade keenly; off since and that form hasn't really worked out, but she's open to improvement and could go well if settling better.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Huge odds and soundly beaten in maidens at Bellewstown (5f, good) and Navan (6f, good); major improvement needed upped further in trip.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Slowly away and never got involved on debut over 6f here (gd-fm) last month when sent off at 28-1; a bit of stamina in pedigree so worth a go at this longer trip, but lots more needed.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan
Spotlight: Promise in all three maidens over 7f; decent third of 20 on handicap debut off a mark of 75 here last month (1m, good), not quite getting home; the drop back to 7f should suit on that evidence, and should be in the mix again returned to maiden company.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Philip Antonacci & Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: E330,000 No Nay Never filly was sent off at 12-1 on debut at Dundalk (6f) last November and finished in mid-field; off since and looks the stable's second string here on rider arrangements.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Cotai Glory filly; E105,000 yearling; closely related to US 5f turf winner Rebel With A Clue, half-sister to three winners inc Cosmic Vega (7f/1m inc 2yo/Listed/Australia; RPR 111) and The X O (6f AW; 106); dam unraced half-sister to 5f Group 2 winner Danehurst; attractively bred and from respected source; check market.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Well-bred daughter of Wootton Bassett has so far failed to live up to her pedigree; rated 76 after three runs in maidens at around 1m2f, the best of which was a 7.25l third of 10 at Leopardstown in May (1m2f, good); markedly dropped in trip now; needs more.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: New Bay filly ran okay without really threatening in her first two maidens, but big step forward at Limerick on latest (7f, soft) when beaten a head by a 77-rated rival; leading form claims on that run, but has to prove she handles this quicker ground (mixed signals on breeding).

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Seventh in barrier trial at Naas last year; promise in a couple of AW maidens over 7f and 1m in the autumn; off since and much more needed on turf debut, but the booking of Oisin Murphy catches the eye (2-5 when riding for this stable in Britain and Ireland); check the market.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Pinatubo filly; unsold E220,000 yearling; half-sister to four winners inc Irish Territory (US 1m turf; RPR 105), Moment Of Silence (7f AW; 99) and Zaragoza (6f; 71); dam Listed-placed 1m2f winner (91), half-sister to 1m2f-1m4f Group 1 winner Ghaiyyath; likely type on paper and in-form yard can ready one.

RaceName: Hailey Equine Irish EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: 150-1 and failed to beat a rival on debut at Navan; seems safe to rule out.

RaceName: BetVictor Steventon Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Success at Meydan early this year was followed by a creditable return to Britain, then a lesser show at Royal Ascot; the same happened in 2025, preceding another below-form effort in July, so that has to be a worry even though best efforts would see him on the premises; William Buick seemingly prefers Al Zanati.

RaceName: BetVictor Steventon Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won his first three races in France last year (twice from the front) and just 9-1 when brought over for the Derby but faded badly into tenth; just useful form since, best of three runs for new yard when 3l fourth of six in a Listed race at Goodwood (1m4f, soft) last time but he needs markedly better than that.

RaceName: BetVictor Steventon Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Went close when bidding for a second win in the Group 3 Diomed at Epsom (8.5f) in June, back to his best; respectable efforts over 1m2f since at Royal Ascot (good to firm; just over 3l ahead of Arabian Light) and Sandown (good) suggest he won't be far away.

RaceName: BetVictor Steventon Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won debut last August and a 1m1f AW Group 3 at Chantilly in November; runner-up in British Group 3s in two other starts, one on good to firm last October and the other in Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f) this April; that's not the best form in this field and he wasn't so good in the Dante at York in May on latest outing, but this lightly raced 3yo could still bring potential and William Buick seemingly prefers him to Arabian Light.

RaceName: BetVictor Steventon Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Group 2 Great Voltigeur entry; French Group 3 winner (1m, soft) last September; well behind Al Zanati in the Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f, good) on reappearance but resumed improvement with a big run at 80-1 in Group 3 at Royal Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) one month ago, keeping on admirably for a close fourth of ten; better still may be needed in today's first-time headgear but he's in calculations.

RaceName: Hadwins Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Quite useful 6yo who made all and won comfortably at Wexford (2m4f, good) a fortnight ago; that first hurdling success (his eighth attempt) was somewhat overdue and his overall record is not entirely convincing but, nevertheless, he'll be hard to beat if all goes to plan here.

RaceName: Hadwins Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bumper winner who came second on first two hurdling starts (2m1f) and didn't need to improve to justify skinny odds at Warwick (2m3f, good) in May; upped in trip again here and probably still has more to offer.

RaceName: Hadwins Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Old Gold Racing With Francesca Cumani
Spotlight: Won readily by nearly 5l at Perth (2m4f, good) in April, her second start over hurdles, and was not discredited when very close second at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month; upped in trip here; ought to be right in the thick of things again.

RaceName: Hadwins Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Only beaten about 8l when fourth of eight at Hexham (2m4f, good; first-time tongue-tie) last month, her second hurdle run, but that form looks modest she has a fair bit to find here.

RaceName: Hadwins Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Joined Dianne Sayer after winning mares' maiden on her seventh start pointing in Ireland; tailed off here (2m1f, good) on rules debut three weeks ago; upped in trip with hood added today.

RaceName: Hadwins Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed little in two points this spring and was well beaten over C&D (good) on rules debut three weeks ago.

RaceName: Dobsons Gaskets EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mrs Anne Atkinson And H D Atkinson
Spotlight: Belied 28-1 odds to win on debut at Redcar (5f, good) four weeks ago; his stable is not known for first-time-out 2yo winners so this £80,000 yearling could have a very bright future; major player under a penalty.

RaceName: Dobsons Gaskets EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 12-1 when fourth of seven, beaten just over 8l, on debut at Beverley (5f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; others have much better form but he's a close relation to a useful sprinter and his trainer's 2yos can improve upon a bundle for their first starts; he's not ruled out.

RaceName: Dobsons Gaskets EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: £50,000 breeze-up buy who showed promise and pace when 16-1 and beaten just over 1l in last of four on recent debut at Ayr (6f, good); this half-brother to four winners is open to improvement and could be a contender.

RaceName: Dobsons Gaskets EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Built upon her debut second at Catterick (5f, good to firm) when easily making all there (5f, good to firm) last Wednesday; she could have more left in the tank and holds leading claims under a penalty.

RaceName: Dobsons Gaskets EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mpr, A & Glo Outlaw & K Quinn
Spotlight: £70,000 breeze-up buy who was 18-1 for last month's debut at Carlisle but showed promise to finish second of four; she could be sharper for that experience and it will be no surprise if she's in the mix.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Best on good ground as he showed when running away with the Sussex Champion Hurdle at Plumpton in April; however, that came at a cost to the tune of 10lb and he failed to fire behind Moon Chime in the Swinton; others might be better in here.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson
Spotlight: 2-3 in bumpers and 5-11 over hurdles after leading late on in the Swinton (good); that was in a first-time tongue-tie that was also on when winning a 2m AW handicap in late May; off just 4lb higher than at Haydock, he's very much in the mix.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Record of 4-7 over hurdles but this is tougher than usual and he could have run a lot better back from a break at Newton Abbot; others appeal more.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who makes his handicap debut after easy odds-on wins on good ground at Warwick and Stratford, posting an eye-catching RPR last time when barely put through the gears; could easily be on a lenient mark.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Front runner; 2-8 over hurdles and returns to this discipline off a good mark having completed a hat-trick over fences three weeks ago off 4lb higher; has found his best form since joining this yard in January and this track should suit him well.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Three hurdle wins at around 2m; has been running well in defeat this summer (2m-2m4f), mostly in a hood but in no headgear last time; cheekpieces now added; others are more compelling.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful Flat form in France; after winning a maiden hurdle for Willie Mullins he was essentially disappointing under both codes and has got nothing going for Dan Skelton, with his latest run after wind surgery at Newton Abbot thoroughly underwhelming (8-1 and weak in the betting); very much the stable second string.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Contrasting performances at Newton Abbot last month, winning well but then finishing last of four; cheekpieces fitted for this much tougher test.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Changed hands in March after winning a claimer; only four ran when a half-length runner-up here a month ago; opposable at this level.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 1m4f winner who has gone 3-4 over hurdles for Tom Lacey and easily won on handicap debut last time at Southwell (good; second run in this headgear combination), albeit against only four rivals; up 8lb but has momentum behind him.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Back from a long absence to justify favouritism at Perth in April (2m4f, good) where she came from off the pace to hit the front when left clear at the last; up 6lb but consistent in the main and wouldn't be exposed just yet.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Lightly raced winning hurdler in France; had a wind operation prior to posting career-best form when a fast-closing second at Ascot in March; up 4lb but has his age allowance now tackling his elders and also the assistance of Harry Cobden.

RaceName: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful Flat performer and also 2-15 as a hurdler (regularly placed); left Harry Fry for £15,000 and was a 6l third over 1m4f at York on last week's stable debut (no headgear); first-time visored now (effective in tongue-tie/cheekpieces).

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Hot To Trot Racing 2 & J Fill
Spotlight: Lightly-raced British challenger is a three-time winner; 2-4 on turf, the wins coming off 81 in a Salisbury handicap in May (1m2f, gd-fm) and a Listed race at Haydock later that month (10.5f, gd-sft); 3.75l fourth of seven in a Group 3 on the AW at Newcastle last time; place claims.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: British raider is talented on her day; two-time winner at Listed level, but has come up well short in a trio of Group 2s this season, most recently when beaten 12.5l in the Duke Of Cambridge at Royal Ascot (1m, gd-fm); needs a revival stepped back up in trip.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: One of two for last year's winning yard; won on her second start for Andre Fabre last year; bought for E125,000 in December and has won back-to-back Listed races on her first two starts for Paddy Twomey, over 1m4f at Cork (sft-hvy) and 1m6f at Gowran (gd-yld); up in class  and drops back in trip; might prefer a bit of an ease in the ground.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: German recruit is 1-11 but has run some cracking races in defeat; respectable Listed run here over 1m4f (good) on first run for this stable; even better when just 0.5l behind Oaks contender Sparan Nua in 1m4f Cork Group 3 (good) on latest, when conceding lots of weight; non-runner on account of good to firm ground here last month, however, and similar conditions likely again.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Five wins from 16 starts, the most recent coming in this race a year ago; fine efforts in defeat in both starts this term, in a Group 2 here over 1m and when beaten 1.5l into second behind the talented Estrange in the Pretty Polly last time over C&D (gd-fm); may prove hard to beat with a repeat of that.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: British raider is 2-6, her wins coming last season in a Yarmouth novice and a class 4 handicap at York off 83; signed off with a Listed third in Italy and ran a blinder on her seasonal return in the Kensington Palace Handicap at Royal Ascot when not getting the clearest of runs; her official rating of 92 may underestimate her and she could outrun her likely odds.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Sole success came in a 3yo Dundalk maiden early last year; 0-10 in stakes races and yet to trouble the judge, though was only 1.75l behind the winner in a 1m Listed race here on latest (good); more needed upped in trip and grade.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: C&D maiden winner on yielding to soft last September; good third on seasonal return in Listed heat at Gowran (9.5f, good to yielding) but soundly beaten in another Listed race at Naas on latest (1m1f, good); moves up in grade and trip; new headgear combination needs to eke out more.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Only 1-10 for Dermot Weld but was highly tried on occasions; made most from wide draw in major handicaps on both starts for Richard Hughes, reeled in late on when third in the Chester Cup (2m2f, good) but backpedalling before the home straight at Royal Ascot (1m6f, good to firm); big shout if he reverts to the Chester performance.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: J William Rowley & S P C Woods
Spotlight: Progressive on AW last autumn and he's been a bit better this season too; won five-runner race at Goodwood (upped to 2m, good) in May and, having been mid-division at Royal Ascot (2m4f), made most when fifth of 20 in the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m, AW); in the mix.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: With tongue-tie enlisted he's hit major new heights for new yard this year, with two wins preceding his excellent fifth of 15 in the 2m2f Chester Cup; comes from off the pace but never got seriously involved wearing first-time cheekpieces when ninth of 20 as second favourite in the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m); needs to bounce back from that pretty disappointing show.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Maiden winner who's been on the premises in all his five handicaps, albeit managing only sixth of 20 from midfield when clear favourite for the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (upped to 2m) last time on AW debut; no handicap win but he's been pretty reliable and this race is a lot less competitive.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Pretty consistent in seven AW runs and well backed when leading final 1f to win the 17-runner Chester Plate (2m2f, good) in May on return to turf; failed to live up to favouritism in another consolation race at Newcastle (2m Vase, AW) three weeks ago but that sixth of 20 off this mark was his best form, so he remains one to be interested in.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)
Spotlight: Gained a deserved first success of the season in three-runner handicap over C&D (good to firm; from Almuhit) last month and got up close home in nine-runner race at Kempton (2m, AW) ten days ago; up 3lb to career-high mark but he's on a roll.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Made most for two game wins in four-runner C&D races (good to firm) last summer, latterly in this race off 2lb higher than today; second to Baileys Khelstar over C&D on latest outing was in just a three-runner race but easily his best Flat effort this year, so he could be on his way back.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Easy to back for Doncaster debut (7f, good to firm; 22-1) in June where she faded in the closing stages and was beaten nearly 6l; has more than 4l to make up on the stronger-finishing third China In Your Hand and, although there's improvement to come, a turnaround looks unlikely.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: C Wright Mrs C Corbett G Hargreave
Spotlight: 11-1 for Doncaster debut (7f, good to firm) one month ago, where she raced freely and had to be switched under 2f out but put in some sterling late work into third of 13 to a more experienced winner; should have plenty to offer this time.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 58,000euros yearling; second foal; Ghaiyyath half-sister to Group-placed 6f/7f winner Royalty Bay (including 2yo; RPR 94); dam 7f/1m AW winner (73), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Go Bears Go; likely type on pedigree and market can guide.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 47,000gns yearling; by Sioux Nation; seventh foal; closely related to US 5f/5.5f turf 2yo winner Amanzi Yimpilo (including stakes), half-sister to 7f winner Rain Or Shine (AW; 60); yard's sole 2yo turf winner this year (out of 31) had had a run and others make more paper appeal.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Bred with stamina in mind on both sides of her pedigree so no surprise she never showed on her 6f debut at Newbury (50-1) 16 days ago; the extra furlong is likely to suit but probably not enough.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Smart pedigree and she was sent off favourite for her 7f debut on Kempton AW 19 days ago, only to finish well adrift; may have had a hidden excuse and interesting to see if her supporters come back for more.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 23,000euros yearling; eleventh foal; Space Traveller half-sister to seven winners including Leshlaa (7f-1m2f including 2yo/Turkish Listed; RPR 114) and Mulzamm (7f-8.5f including 2yo/AW; 106); dam 1m2f AW winner (82); trainer has a useful strike-rate with the few 2yos he's run this year, including with newcomers; check the betting.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: First foal; by Bangkok out of bumper, 2m hurdle and 1m4f AW (RPR 82) winner from just four starts, half-sister to winners Tabjeel (1m2f AW), Zaaneh (7f/8.6f) and Bairam (6f 2yo); first impressions suggest longer trips may be needed but the betting should be helpful; leading yard won this in 2024.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Big prices and not yet achieved much, in 5f and 7f maidens at Leicester.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 9-1 for 6f debut on Lingfield AW in June, when showing some mid-race promise before dropping out; may be more of a handicap prospect.

RaceName: Cartmel Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles but made the frame on last three attempts, losing out only by a short head on one occasion; placed twice on the Flat lately will be suited by today's trip/ground; good chance.

RaceName: Cartmel Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Four-time Flat winner who finished third in both his handicap hurdles, the latter at Market Rasen (2m, good) in May; seemed to give the leaders far too much rope when beaten at skinny odds on the Flat on Wednesday but that run was still good enough to confirm his wellbeing; shortlisted.

RaceName: Cartmel Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran well for a long way when third of seven on latest hurdling start, at Doncaster (2m, soft) in February, and will be fine on the faster ground here but recent Flat form has been underwhelming; others appeal more.

RaceName: Cartmel Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Launched handicap career with two respectable runs (both 2m, good) towards end of last season but, following a wind op, took backward step when safely held at Uttoxeter on Wednesday; sports refitted hood here; improvement required.

RaceName: Cartmel Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-11 over hurdles and prone to clumsy jumping; belied 33-1 odds when second at Perth (2m, good) last month and didn't run badly over 2m4f there 16 days ago but others look less risky.

RaceName: Cartmel Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Came from off the pace to win C&D (good) selling handicap for George Bewley in May, her tenth hurdling start, and also ran well when third here (2m6f, good) on last month's stable debut; this drop back in trip is probably the right move; ought to make her presence felt.

RaceName: Cartmel Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Hambleton Racing Mrs C Clark Miss A Lee
Spotlight: Fair Flat racer with three middle-distance wins on his CV; yet to show any significant promise after four hurdle starts this year but has lowly mark for this handicap debut and some of that Flat ability may yet transfer to this discipline; watch the betting.

RaceName: Cartmel Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran well to a point when fourth in C&D selling handicap won by Alvesta in May but was beaten about 19l in the end and has been safely held in two subsequent chases; 3lb wrong today and now 0-40 under rules.

RaceName: Simon's 20 Year Heart Transplant Anniversary Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten eight times and the drop back to 6f isn't an obvious positive, but he's run some good races and was third in a handicap at Catterick (7f, good to firm) last Wednesday; prominent in calculations.

RaceName: Simon's 20 Year Heart Transplant Anniversary Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: He has a useful pedigree and cost 150,000euros at the breeze-ups last May; well-beaten last of five at 66-1 on debut at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) last month but he made the running in a hotter race than this; drops back in trip and interesting to see if he's stronger in the betting.

RaceName: Simon's 20 Year Heart Transplant Anniversary Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up on his first two starts this season (6f, good to firm) and a good case can be made on that form; however, he's gone off the boil on his three outings since and needs to turn things around.

RaceName: Simon's 20 Year Heart Transplant Anniversary Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in C&D handicap (good) in May on stable/seasonal debut and that form puts him in the picture; however, disappointing at Redcar (5f, good) since and now 0-10; needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Simon's 20 Year Heart Transplant Anniversary Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Encouraging second on debut over C&D (good) in May and built on that when again finishing runner-up at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm); this well-bred filly could continue to progress and she's a leading contender.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: C Nightingale & Hold My Beer Partnership
Spotlight: Very capable prominent racer on his day and he had one of those in winning by 2l over C&D a month ago; 4lb rise elevates him to a career-high mark and he won't be the only one putting pace to the race.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Two good runs in defeat at 3m before coming down at the Canal Turn in the Topham Trophy; went close to winning this two years ago and that was after a two-month break; definite possibilities, as this mark isn't beyond him; Harry Cobden has won the last three renewals of this race.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: His defeat of the smart Jesse Evans at Sligo in October heightens interest and new headgear is tried after two lesser efforts, particularly the latest; he has become hard to predict but it's unwise to put a line through him.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Wasn't disgraced in finishing third of six over 2m4f at Uttoxeter on stable debut but he's not as good as he was and an 11yo now.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Win/placed form in his usual cheekpieces before making all for a narrow success in the newly fitted visor (with tongue-tie worn for last seven starts) at Cartmel (2m5f, good); he's getting high in the weights now (raised another 4lb) and he won't be the only one racing prominently.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ex-Gordon Elliott; made a successful handicap chase debut for this yard at Ffos Las (2m3f, soft) in January but up and down since, with recent thirds just okay efforts (latest in this new headgear combination when behind Breizh River); may prefer softer ground ideally.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 1-7 over fences; gifted a walkover before finishing second in a four-runner handicap at Uttoxeter where he failed to fire when favourite for their Summer Cup 20 days ago; he's better than that and further progress is possible with cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful Flat and hurdles performer who has gone 0-5 over fences since a winning start in May 2025 and he'll need to run better than he did in a handicap chase at Ballinrobe last time; however, a case can be made off this mark on best form; versatile trip-wise from 2m to beyond 3m.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Clearcut winner on chase debut (2m4f, good) on final run for Rebecca Menzies and similar sort of form in defeat in two runs for the Skeltons, last time pushing earlier-race contender Newton Rambler all the way back over 2m at Uttoxeter; returning to further looks a plus and this hold-up performer should have this run to suit him.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner who has won four times on the Flat since moving to current yard earlier this year; lacks recent match practice over jumps and that could count against him in this competitive a race.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: In a solid run of form but this is a tough mark for an exposed 9yo and he's undergone a fourth wind operation since finishing fifth at Perth in early June.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Three wins at around 2m4f last season, all in small fields on good ground; after some blips he got himself back on track when second to a well-handicapped horse over C&D a month ago on first visit here; likely to run well with Shane Fenelon now taking off 5lb; one of three runners for a yard that's captured this eight times.

RaceName: Betway Summer Plate Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Consistent chaser who was better than ever in dominating a small field at Fakenham two runs back (2m5f185yds, good); didn't run badly in defeat last time and off a light weight he could have a part to play.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Has recorded six of his eight wins over 5f, including last year's Rockingham Handicap here; proved that he is in good shape with a conditions race victory at Naas a fortnight ago; has run well in Listed events this term; trainer has a strong contender in Headmaster.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Two handicap wins this season, over 5f here (sft-hvy) and over the same trip at Navan on (good); virtually inseparable from Tango Flare based on Listed third behind course specialist here last time; both could feature.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Beat course specialist Big Gossey when winning the Bold Lad Handicap here last September; no luck in running in two handicaps this term; won a Fairyhouse conditions race for the third time in four years before a fine second to Big Gossey in a Listed race here last time, a whisker in front of Bodhi Bear.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Best known for AW exploits, also effective on turf; second to Bodhi Bear in a 5f handicap at Navan; visor now replaces blinkers following a below-par Dundalk run last Sunday.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up in the Ayr Silver Cup (6f, soft) last September when first home on his side; repeated a 2025 Epsom win (6f, soft) early last month; first of his small group when ninth in the Wokingham; generally dependable sort; stable won this in 2016.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Richard Hannon; off the mark for the Quinn team when making virtually all in a 22-runner handicap at York Dante meeting (6f, good); bounced straight back from a lesser run at Thirsk when second at Ripon; made all for a 6f Chester win three weeks ago; stable won this with Mr Wagyu in 2022.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Smart turf form at up to 7f in Bahrain for George Scott early last year; some good runs this year, second to Sir Les Patterson on the AW (6f) in February and close fourth at Chester in May (7.5f, good); no luck in running in the Habitat here (won by Headmaster), again behind that rival at Cork.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Added to two British 7f wins when bagging the Habitat Handicap (6f, good) here in May on second start for new yard; had difficulty finding a gap before storming home to win by a neck with several of these behind; another solid display over 7f at Cork; top trainer of sprinters won this in 2020 and 2021.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
OwnerName: J Fairhurst & Damian McCann & Cyril McMullan
Spotlight: Has become highly dependable since being gelded, winning three of his last eight starts, including two on AW in Britain; leading contender after Rockingham third with several of these rivals behind him; overall form favours him over the winner Genesis.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: All five wins have come on the AW, including two at 6f last winter; highlight of turf form when third to Tango Flare here last autumn; fair form this term, but needs first-time cheekpieces to have an improving effect.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice last year over 5f at Cork and also finished second to Keke here in the Rockingham, a fine effort for a horse of his age; not at his best at Dundalk on first start this year; down the field behind Genesis in the Rockingham last month.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins this season have been gained over 7f; asserted late in a seven-runner contest at Fairyhouse ten days ago; may find some of these rivals too speedy.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Dual AW winner who got off the mark on turf in a 6f handicap here in May; badly hampered in the race won by Headmaster later that month; 3lb wrong when taking the 5f Rockingham Handicap here last time; stiff task off his new rating,

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up six times before belatedly opening his account in a premier handicap over 6f here; fifth in this race last year; two satisfactory runs this term; could go well, but a record of one win from 22 starts makes it hard to be overly confident.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Stable second-string when landing a 7f maiden at Fairyhouse on his third start; added to his score by beating Gavoo in a two-horse race at Naas; still relatively unexposed.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins last season, a Listowel maiden and a 6f Dundalk event; beaten by the odds-on Oro Blanco in a two-horse race last time; previously unplaced favourite in a 7f handicap here.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins have come at this track but over 6f/7f; this season's form linked to Genesis and Headmaster puts her in the mix, although several others would seem to have marginally stronger claims.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-time turf winner, including a 6f Group 3 at this venue in 2023, Listed contests and this event 12 months ago; was once rated 108, so has dropped a long way; inconsistent this year; good run when second here last time, 3lb wrong

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: 7f maiden winner at Limerick last season on his sixth and final start for Aidan O'Brien; went close in a 6f handicap at Navan three starts ago; 4lb wrong, up against it.

RaceName: Liverpool FC Foundation Scurry Handicap
Spotlight: Lacks an outing this year and is 6lb out of the handicap; signed off last year with 6f AW maiden win; gelded since; rider's claim the main plus.

RaceName: Hallgarten And Novum Wines Hackwood Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Broadly progressive 4yo whose form in notable handicaps suggests he could develop into a Group horse; went close on reappearance in his bid for a York double, then finished a respectable sixth when favourite for the Wokingham at Royal Ascot; remains of major interest.

RaceName: Hallgarten And Novum Wines Hackwood Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Has an underwhelming strike-rate for a horse of his ability and losing spell goes back over two years; that said, has spent over half of his career in Group 1 races, best effort when third in the 2024 Commonwealth Cup; drops back in class after a heavy defeat at this year's Royal Ascot.

RaceName: Hallgarten And Novum Wines Hackwood Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Very lightly raced in recent times but latest effort, namely fifth in the Wokingham, suggests he retains a very useful level of ability aged seven; however, needs to prove he's still capable of peak form, which includes Group wins at Newbury and the Curragh.

RaceName: Hallgarten And Novum Wines Hackwood Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Clear best in the 2025 Jersey at Royal Ascot (7f) but was pulled up (lost action) in only subsequent start last term and has failed to show his form this year; drop back to 6f had no effect last time; enough to prove.

RaceName: Hallgarten And Novum Wines Hackwood Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Ex-Irish 5yo who has low mileage and progressive form; record since switched to turf and dropped to 6f reads 233; has performed well in Listed event at Salisbury and the Wokingham at Royal Ascot for new yard; already very useful and warrants respect with further progress plausible.

RaceName: Hallgarten And Novum Wines Hackwood Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Still below best when third at Newcastle (AW debut) last time but that was something of resurgence; progressive spell culminated in wins over C&D (Listed) and at Haydock (Group 2) in May 2025; possibilities provided he returns to that sort of form.

RaceName: Hallgarten And Novum Wines Hackwood Stakes (Group 3)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Cxx & M Parker
Spotlight: Sole 3yo in this field; largely progressive colt whose form features wins in York sales race as a 2yo and C&D Listed event on penultimate outing; creditable seventh in the Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot latest; may have more to offer back down in class; major player.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Returned with a win in 1m4f Thirsk handicap (good; first-time tongue-tie) in May; creditable effort when sixth of nine in a Listed race at Pontefract (1m4f, good again) since but the reopposing Revoir was 4.5l ahead in second; improvement needed at this level.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has developed into quite a useful handicapper on AW but will need to raise her game considerably back on turf if she's to play a leading role in this company.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who made a creditable return to action when 4l fifth (Revoir 3.75l ahead in second) in 1m4f Pontefract Listed event (good) four weeks ago, staying on after getting outpaced; entitled to come on for the run.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Progressed into a useful handicapper since last autumn; couldn't cope with a steep rise in class when 9l fourth in 1m3f Carlisle Group 3 (good to firm) seven weeks ago but it was a good race for the grade (won by Estrange) and the way she stuck on suggests 1m4f should be within range even though she's a bit of a keen goer.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Posted her first triple-digit RPR when landing 1m4f Goodwood handicap (good) on seasonal return; the form of that race was boosted by the runner-up winning a Listed race next time but she couldn't uphold it herself when behind a few of these in Pontefract Listed event four weeks ago, although the way she weakened quickly late on suggests something may not have been right.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Record of 0-9 since debut win but her Listed record reads 252222, going down by only a neck at Pontefract (1m4f, good; three of today's rivals behind) most recently; due a change of luck.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Confirmed debut promise when landing short odds in novice at Haydock (1m2f, soft) eight weeks ago, with reopposing stablemate Tribal Queen (won next time) just under 3l back in third; this is a jump in class but the Haydock form has also been boosted by the runner-up and her leading stable has won this with a lightly raced 3yo twice in recent years; hooded first time; interesting.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Sister to connections' Dante/Voltiguer winner Pride Of Arras; comfortable win at Southwell (1m, AW) last November on sole 2yo start; found the drop to 7f against her in Fred Darling on reappearance and left that well behind when third to Legacy Link in the Musidora at York (extended 1m2f, good) in May; never involved in the Oaks at Epsom since but retains potential with her sights lowered.

RaceName: Ric And Mary Hambro Aphrodite Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Got the hang of things late when just under 3l behind Fashion's Fancy on her 1m2f Haydock debut and made all with ease at Kempton (1m3f) 19 days later; has a similar profile to her half-sister, Spirited Style, who won this race last year on her third start.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Built upon pleasing stable debut when coming from off the pace for C&D (good) success under Leah Noreci in May; subsequent 3lb rise ought to be manageable and she can make her presence felt again.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Progressive on the Flat and over hurdles last year, collecting eight wins (one over C&D) in total; absent since respectable fifth in Scottish County Hurdle at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in January, though, and current mark demands another personal best.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up (albeit no match for winner) off today's mark at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) in May and didn't run badly on the Flat (1m6f) last month; could yet have a bigger effort in him over jumps but this drop back in trip may not be in his favour.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Ex-Irish 6yo who is 2-3 in maiden/novice hurdles for new stable this season, the latter win gained by 18l at Uttoxeter (2m, good; first-time hood) 11 days ago; faces tougher opposition on this British handicap debut but still commands respect.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Won a juvenile hurdle for Gavin Cromwell at Punchestown (2m, good to yielding) in October and was also on target in 2m2f Flat marathon for new stable in June; still unexposed over jumps; no surprise if he features.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in a Listed bumper last season and 2-3 since switched to hurdles this spring, having beaten subsequent wide-margin winner Eternal Angel at Uttoxeter on latest outing; must be considered on handicap debut.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Appreciated drop to 0-110 grade when second under Amber Jackson-Fennell at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good; wore cheekpieces) last month but is not as good as he was and needs change of headgear to give him a boost here.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
OwnerName: T Alderson & Ales And Dales Syndicate
Spotlight: Won four-runner Sedgefield maiden (2m4f, soft) in December and remains unexposed under rules but his next two hurdle runs were disappointing and he unseated rider mid-race on last month's chase/handicap debut; dropped in trip and reverts to hurdling today; others have more obvious claims.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Not quite at his best this year but last month's Hexham third was respectable and she remains only 2lb higher than when beating Saligo Bay to win this race under Thomasina Eyston in 2025; can't be ruled out.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Bounced back to form with narrow win at Kelso (2m5f, good) in May but that seems to be his favourite track and he ran poorly here (2m6f) last month; this drop back in trip is not ideal; not the percentage call.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Professional Jockeys & Amateur Riders' Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up three consecutive times at Hexham (2m-2m4f, good) this spring and also suited by this track but his latest run was below par and he's 2lb wrong today.

RaceName: Ripon Bell-Ringer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Down the field at York and Windsor on his last two starts but he's won on his last four visits to Ripon (1m2f, good/good to soft), including twice this year; it's surprising he's only ever had one crack at 1m4f, given how well he kept on over that trip in 2024; firmly in calculations back here.

RaceName: Ripon Bell-Ringer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 8yo who has been beaten 23 times since his last win in September 2024 and failed to threaten at the Curragh three weeks ago; however, prior to that this Irish raider was second at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm) behind a subsequent Royal Ascot winner, and he's in the picture on that form.

RaceName: Ripon Bell-Ringer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Ten-time AW winner who is 5lb lower than when winning at Southwell (1m4f, AW) last November; however, this is his first run since January and he was well beaten at Ascot in May 2024 on his last encounter with turf; 0-5 on grass and others have less to prove.

RaceName: Ripon Bell-Ringer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Disappointing at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm) a fortnight ago but prior to that he was 2-2 since being gelded, with convincing wins at Southwell (1m3f, AW) and Hamilton (1m3f, good); could bounce back.

RaceName: Ripon Bell-Ringer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: He's edged down the weights for his new yard this season and has been placed off this mark on his last two starts, finishing third at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) then beaten just a neck at Hamilton (1m5f, good); back up in grade this afternoon but he might not be far away.

RaceName: Ripon Bell-Ringer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 5yo mare whose form has gone to a new level this season, with wins at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) and Goodwood (1m4f, good to soft) sandwiching a runner-up finish at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm); she may be the type who only just does enough and therefore she could have more still left in the tank.

RaceName: Ripon Bell-Ringer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: He's won just one of his last 23 starts and has failed to fire on his last two outings; however, he had a tough assignment in the John Smith's Cup at York last Saturday and he's dropped to a handy mark; runner-up to the 4-7 favourite in this race last year and he's not ruled out.

RaceName: Ripon Bell-Ringer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 3yo who has improved with each of his four starts and finished a close second of five on handicap debut at Thirsk (1m4f, good to firm) this month; the winner has followed up and this progressive colt could play a leading role following a 1lb rise.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Small fields suit this free-going mare, as do sharp, flat tracks; perhaps on a fairly tough mark after coming up short last time but shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Only win in 12 hurdle starts was a maiden; likes to crack on with things and probably went off a bit too hot when a well-held fourth at Cartmel (2m1f; after 99 days off); has another front-runner to deal with in Hello Sweety, but the ability is there to win off this mark.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Race-fit off the Flat when a battling winner over a little shorter at Worcester a month ago (visor back on) and looks a good thing to give his running once more off 4lb higher; respected.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual AW winner who is 0-7 over hurdles; good first run in cheekpieces when second at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft) but never got involved the last time (2m6f); perhaps a fast-run race back at this distance will benefit her.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Classy juvenile and a six-time winner, including four times at this track over 5f/6f; two wins last season included a Listed heat over 5f at Naas and the Group 3 Phoenix Sprint Stakes here over 6f (both on good); weakened late over 6f in a Group 2 here last time, and not dismissed back at 5f.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: British raider was in fine form at Meydan at the turn of the year, winning a Listed (yielding) and a Group 2 (good), both over 5f; didn't get the smoothest of runs when beaten 3.5l in the King Charles III at Royal Ascot last time (behind Rosy Affair and Jakajaro) and could get closer here if getting a clear passage.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Prominent racer started his campaign with back-to-back handicap wins at Newmarket (5f, good) and York (5f, good); yet to score at Pattern level but huge runs the last twice in the Group 2 Temple Stakes at Haydock (5f, good) and when a 1.5l fifth of 26 in the King Charles III (5f, gd-fm) at Royal Ascot; fourth in this last year and should be in the mix again.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
OwnerName: Miss Kate McGivern & Thomas Hore & Shane M Power
Spotlight: Six-time handicap winner (including over C&D); took his form to a new level when winning a Listed heat at Cork (5f, good) last month when wearing this headgear combination for the first time; this is much harder, so another step forward is required.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: British challenger a five-time handicap winner, most recently scoring off 92 at York in May;  creditable first run in stakes company in a Sandown Group 3 last time when a 1.25l third behind the 110-rated Rumstar; that's solid form and Colin Keane keeps the faith; place chance.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Consistent British raider boasts a decent strike rate of 5-13 but her last four wins have come over 6f; finished a 1.5l fourth (a nose ahead of Jakajaro) over 5f in the King Charles III at Royal Ascot last time and there shouldn't be much between them again here; big player.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: 3yo son of Wootton Bassett signed off last term with an excellent second at the Breeders' Cup (5f, firm) but form has been regressive this season, albeit all three runs have come over 6f; the drop back to 5f is a positive, but he has a fair bit to find on the figures and Ryan Moore prefers the chances of Charles Darwin.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: 3yo won three of his four starts last season, including the Norfolk Stakes (5f, gd-fm); started off this campaign with a Listed win at Naas (6f, gd-yld) but has questions to answer after finishing well beaten in a Group 3 back at Naas and in the Commonwealth Cup in first-time blinkers (retained) on latest; the pick of Moore, but needs to get back on track down in trip.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: 3yo filly is 3-9 with all three of her wins coming over 5f/6f at Naas; won a Group 3 over the latter trip on her seasonal return (good) and outran her odds of 28-1 when a fine 2l fourth in the Commonwealth Cup (6f, gd-fm); gets all the allowances here and the drop back to 5f should pose no problems; big chance.

RaceName: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Sapphire Stakes (Group 2)
OwnerName: Stonestreet Stables, LLC & AMO Racing Limited
Spotlight: Speedy two-time Listed winner (including over C&D); yet to win this season; beaten off 95 in a handicap over C&D in June and again off 1lb lower in the Palace Of Holyrood at Royal Ascot on latest; much more needed upped in grade.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Faces a stiff task off top weight in this field, based on his spring form; however, may improve for physical tweak (gelded since last run) and he represents a yard that has an excellent record in the Super Sprint; market support should be heeded.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Won on debut then ran well when favourite for the Lily Agnes at Chester; however, his form has dipped markedly in two starts since, gelded prior to nursery defeat latest; huge turnaround is required.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Failed to beat a rival at Royal Ascot but is a progressive 5f filly otherwise and registered a half-length success in Sandown Listed event (good) most recently, taking record to 2-4; sole attempt under William Buick resulted in the earlier win; leading player even with a 5lb penalty.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Four-race maiden whose form includes a couple of second-place finishes but who hasn't progressed and still needs to learn to relax, having failed to settle when posting her lesser efforts.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Started favourite for two Leicester maidens (5f, good to firm) in the spring, making all and nicely on top at the finish last time; comes back fresh for a crack at this bigger prize, looks likely to show further improvement and represents a trainer who has a growing tally in sales races; interesting contender.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Represents a trainer who landed this prize in 2022; campaigned non-domestically since scoring at Redcar on debut; ran well in French Listed races (5f) the last twice, good third at Deauville latest occasion; major contender provided that form is transferred back to home soil.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Proving consistent, as a series of placed efforts reflect; however, chance of overcoming a difficult task at the weights depends on the effects of first-time headgear and return to 5f for first time since debut.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Ran as if something perhaps went amiss when co-favourite for the Sandown Listed contest won by Bint Archange last time; now goes in tongue-tie and may resume the steady progress that culminated in a Bath maiden success (5.7f, good); remains of interest.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Absent and gelded since a rather disappointing effort in the Lily Agnes at Chester, failing to back up April promise that featured a comfortable win at Wolverhampton (5f, AW); something to prove.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has consistent form but played second fiddle in a Goodwood seller (5f) most recently and looks well out of her depth in this much deeper company.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Has posted similar efforts in 5f novice events, finishing fourth both times, latest at Windsor; a nursery would have been more suitable than this assignment, as a mark of 65 reflects.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Gradually getting the hang of things but RPRs in the 50s reflect that he holds modest claims in this field and would have been more interesting in a low-grade handicap.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Gives the impression she may benefit from this drop in distance but not the rise in grade; holds very weak claims on her 6f form.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Gained valuable experience for a scenario of this nature by finishing second in big field at Newbury (6f, good to firm; well-supported favourite) last week, backing up the promise of his eye-catching effort at Windsor (5f); still open to progress and looks likely to go well.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Won at Thirsk (5f) on debut; down the field in notable races since, latest at Royal Ascot, and still needs to prove she's capable of progress; however, these terms may enable her to prove resurgent.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Has shown significant promise in Goodwood races, finishing second over 5f (good) then going one better in clearcut fashion over 6f (soft); open to further improvement and is the Hannon first string on form and jockey arrangements; yard has a great record in this race; shortlisted.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: By a Coventry winner for his connections; posted a promising effort in tight finish at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) where he split a couple of rivals who had the benefit of experience; one of the bigger players at the weights and has possibilities with progress on the cards.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Harry Angel filly who is hitherto her trainer's only 2yo runner this year and bids to take her record to 2-2; won only an ordinary maiden at Chepstow (5f, soft) but is open to progress and gets in here off an attractive weight; one to consider.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Similar type to stablemate Final Appeal, being a thrice-raced contender who proved disappointing when last seen; however, made all at Thirsk (5f, good) the time before and may still do better; rating suggests she's no forlorn hope at the weights.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Ran creditably at Yarmouth last week when bidding for a 5f nursery double; beat Past Passion at Nottingham the time before; remains in form but faces a much stiffer assignment on these terms.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Maintained consistency by finishing a good second to Holi Scarlett in Nottingham nursery last time; however, both fillies face a markedly tougher task in this field.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Has failed to show progress in two starts since prevailing narrowly at Musselburgh (5f); fitting of hood needs to have a major effect on these difficult terms.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Justified favouritism with something to spare at Yarmouth (good to firm) in latest 6f start, building on her debut promise; gives the impression she'll be as effective over 5f and should have more to offer; helps to give her connections, who also run Etienne, an interesting hand.

RaceName: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBBRace)
Spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who posted clear best effort in sole AW attempt; has something to prove and a lot to find.

RaceName: Lettergold Plastics Handicap
Spotlight: On a three-year losing run and nothing like the force of old but his current mark reflects that and this is a good bit easier than the two handicaps he tackled in the spring; no surprise were this class dropper to go well with his yard among the winners.

RaceName: Lettergold Plastics Handicap
Spotlight: Reacted well to first-time cheekpieces (added to tongue-tie) when edging ahead late on over C&D (good) four weeks ago; not the most convincing (carries head awkwardly under pressure) but he's only been nudged up 2lb.

RaceName: Lettergold Plastics Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer who won two 7f turf handicaps last summer; also close second off 1lb higher at Lingfield (7f) in December but his form dipped in two subsequent starts last winter; off for 169 days so his peak fitness has to be taken on trust.

RaceName: Lettergold Plastics Handicap
OwnerName: J Morley D Reynolds C Watkins & Partner
Spotlight: Threatened on AW and turf in the spring but his last two efforts have been below par (C&D latterly); feasibly handicapped but others are more persuasive.

RaceName: Lettergold Plastics Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't build on a promising start on AW last spring, including well held in 1m Haydock handicap (good to soft) on sole turf start; has been gelded and had a second wind operation ahead of this return from eight months off; it's still early days but his profile isn't compelling.

RaceName: Lettergold Plastics Handicap
OwnerName: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with 6f success at Southwell off 2lb higher; respectable third of ten over 7f at Epsom (good) 17 days ago; successful on his previous outing over C&D; this prominent racer is capable of making a bold bid.

RaceName: Lettergold Plastics Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins on turf have been achieved on this course (7f/1m); beaten around 9l in two comeback outings in recent months but eased 3lb and capable of bouncing back to form to a form with a bang.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Popular veteran who runs this track well and capitalised upon some significant help from the handicapper when winning Norfolk National at Fakenham (3m5f, good) in May; this drop back in trip is not ideal on his return to hurdling, though; cheekpieces (worn for last three races) left off today.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Unraced over hurdles since winning Cheltenham novice in 2024 but recent 2m7f/3m chase form has been very respectable and he's edging down the weights; swaps cheekpieces for first-time blinkers; could have a say.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well below best during five-race campaign last season but took step back in right direction when third over fences at Bangor (2m1f, good) two months ago; remains on good mark for this hurdle return and could plausibly be better suited by this sort of trip nowadays; cheekpieces replace blinkers.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: In good form over fences here since wind op in April, most notably when winning over 3m1f (good) three weeks ago; should be fine back over this hurdles and over this shorter trip; respected in current mood.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner for Warren Greatrex; ran as though something was amiss on latest outing in January and was sold for £10,000 in May but starts off for new stable on workable mark; tongue-tie on; market helpful.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Returned from break with two underwhelming chase runs this season and unseated rider when back over hurdles in plating company 12 days ago; tricky to weigh up at present but represents respected Irish stable.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Came good for current stable at Hexham (2m, good) last month, his second run in cheekpieces; up 4lb to career-high mark but will be suited by this move back up in trip and is evidently in good nick.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: In career-best form this season, most notably when coming from the rear to win Class 2 handicap at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) three weeks ago; still unexposed over this sort of trip; might be the answer.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won twice on good ground this spring (2m7f/2m4f) and last month's close second at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) was a career-best performance; likely contender again here.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Teenager who was useful in his prime and is well handicapped on form as recent as the autumn; returned from break with satisfactory run in June but was well beaten on only previous visit to Cartmel; younger rivals preferred.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Returned from eight-month break in excellent form three weeks ago, winning comfortably by 10l over C&D (good); 8lb higher in higher-grade race today but still considered.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs D Chapman & Mrs E Quinlan
Spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, most recently in May; needs to bounce back from below-par run here three weeks ago but probably still on a workable mark; dangerous to ignore.

RaceName: Cartmel Crystal Cup Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Belied 40-1 odds when creditable third at Kelso (2m5f, good to soft; wore cheekpieces) in March but weakened quickly after making a lot of the running over C&D (good; visored, as today) three weeks ago and is now 0-9 for stable; needs recent wind op to have made a big difference.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
OwnerName: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd
Spotlight: Capitalised on a golden opportunity for easy win at 8-13 in a seller at Beverley (8.4f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; that may have given his confidence a boost and he's unexposed in the retained tongue-tie/cheekpieces, but he's opposable on what he's achieved in handicaps this season.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: 7yo whose losing sequence goes back 21 races to September 2024 but he went close behind Yermanthere over C&D (good) last month and was a good third at Ayr (1m2f, good) last time; might not be far away.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: 8yo who capitalised on a reduced mark to win over C&D (good) last month before 7l second to easy winner in amateur jockeys' race at Pontefract (1m2f, good); he's not ruled out back here.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in May on second start and won a novice at Salisbury (1m2f, good) last month; her latest form looks solid and this unexposed handicap newcomer could continue to progress; player.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Often ran well in 2yo campaign and he got off the mark on his reappearance in a novice at Redcar (7f, good to firm); it's possible he'll stay this far but there is a question mark on that score, and also one about his current form as he's been well beaten on handicaps on his last two starts.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: 11yo who has been up and down this season but has won twice, including over C&D (good), and he ran pretty well when third here (1m, good) last time; finished third in this last year and he could give a good account.

RaceName: GAIN Railway Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Not especially convincing when landing the odds over 5f at Naas on second start; much sharper next time over C&D when second to the subsequent Coventry winner Great Barrier Reef; beaten 7l into ninth after going off 5-2 favourite in the Norfolk; got up late to win a 5f Listed race at Naas a fortnight ago; cheekpieces on now.

RaceName: GAIN Railway Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Refused to enter the stalls on his intended debut at Navan; made a good impression over C&D early last month; missed the break in the Windsor Castle; will need to be a lot sharper.

RaceName: GAIN Railway Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Just denied over 5f here on soft first time out; won with plenty to spare over 6f at Naas; ran respectably, but well below general expectations, when sixth behind stablemate Great Barrier Reef in the Coventry Stakes; needs to redeem himself after odds-on Group 3 defeat behind two De Aguiar-trained runners here three weeks ago; needs to settle better; Moore sticks with him.

RaceName: GAIN Railway Stakes (Group 2)
OwnerName: Ramiro Restrepo & Jacob West & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Won a C&D maiden on debut before a fine effort in the Group 3 Marble Hill Stakes; third behind the subsequent Coventry winner Great Barrier Reef then, 1 1/4l off Carry The Flag; second to a longer-priced stablemate in Group 3 Anglesey Stakes here three weeks ago, a head in front of the odds-on Confucius.

RaceName: GAIN Railway Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Promising debut behind a Ballydoyle-trained colt over C&D; still looked raw when winning narrowly at Fairyhouse; will need to be a lot more streetwise here.

RaceName: GAIN Railway Stakes (Group 2)
Spotlight: Listowel maiden winner on his third outing; out of his depth in the Coventry; 90-rated colt will need others to underperform.

RaceName: GAIN Railway Stakes (Group 2)
OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Mrs A O'Callaghan
Spotlight: Form of debut win at Navan has worked out; finished behind Carry The Flag in the Norfolk; Robson De Aguiar also saddles Immortal Guard, who was involved in the stable's Group 3 one-two here last month.

RaceName: Read Paul Townend's Betway Blog Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won a C&D handicap in April and was runner-up at Southwell last month but he's not easy to predict and was well below form at Stratford last time; that took his chase record to 1-15 and he needs to bounce back again.

RaceName: Read Paul Townend's Betway Blog Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Triple chase winner including over C&D but he's been beaten 35l in both runs since returning from an absence in May; has something to prove and he needs a major revival.

RaceName: Read Paul Townend's Betway Blog Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won an Irish point on yielding ground in March; struggled in hunter chases in next four runs but he was an eye-catching fourth on handicap debut at Wexford (2m3f, good) two weeks ago; needs to build on that but he's still lightly raced and should have more to offer in this sphere.

RaceName: Read Paul Townend's Betway Blog Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Flat winner who is 0-6 over hurdles but he won on chase debut at Fakenham (2m, good) in May before a creditable third at Uttoxeter 11 days ago; did well to hang in there after being pestered for the lead last time and he's been an improved performer since switched to fences; respected.

RaceName: Read Paul Townend's Betway Blog Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing
Spotlight: Ended last year with a brave win at Catterick (1m7f, good to soft) but he's not gone on from that in two runs this summer; needs to raise his game but he's now only 1lb higher than for his last success and his first two chase wins were over C&D (good).

RaceName: Read Paul Townend's Betway Blog Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who scored over fences at Sedgefield (2m, good to soft) last December but he hasn't really fired in his last three runs; chase record is now 1-11 and others are more persuasive.

RaceName: Read Paul Townend's Betway Blog Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-18 under rules but he was an easy winner of an Irish point in May; well held back over regulation fences at Uttoxeter (3m, good) last month but he took a strong hold and ran well to a point in that race; could benefit from this drop back in trip and it wouldn't be a surprise if he was in the mix.

RaceName: Read Paul Townend's Betway Blog Handicap Chase
OwnerName: M Enticott / Ella Pickard Racing Club
Spotlight: Sole success was at Stratford (2m1f, good) last May and she's been out of sorts in her last seven starts; 5lb out of the weights this time and she has a lot to prove.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 370,000gns yearling by St Mark's Basilica, half-sister to the stable's prolific winner Dubai Honour (1m-1m4f including Group 1); 8-1 for novice here (1m1f, good to soft) five weeks ago, coming from midfield to beat Miss Scott by half a length as the pair drew clear; 7lb worse off with that rival today but that debut win for this filly was full of promise.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 650,000gns yearling by Night Of Thunder; readily made a winning debut at Leicester (7f, good to soft) last September; 5-4 favourite when hooded for a novice here (1m1f, good to soft) five weeks ago and always prominent before losing out by half a length; that rival was making her debut but Miss Scott is weighted to turn the tables today.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 85,000euros yearling by Sea The Stars; 80-1 and never dangerous in novice here (1m1f, good to soft) five weeks ago; should improve but she has about 9l to find with Manaar and Miss Scott.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 110,000gns yearling by Dark Angel; 17-2 from 11-1 for a conditions stakes here (7f, good to soft) last September when a close third of seven, always prominent; that was a bright start against fairly useful opposition, which earns respect despite her coming from a speedy family.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 33-1 when sixth of seven at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) in October and last of four at Lingfield (1m2f, good) this May; early days but that's modest maiden form.

RaceName: Club 26 At Ascot Handicap
Spotlight: 6-21 on AW but still seeking a first turf win after 15 attempts; latest Thirsk fourth (6f, good to firm) as good as anything he's produced on the grass but may find at least the odd one too strong again.

RaceName: Club 26 At Ascot Handicap
Spotlight: Beat his 17 rivals at Thirsk (6f, good) in April, his second run for David O'Meara, and there have been a couple of good second-place finishes since, including over C&D (good to firm) a fortnight ago; a bold show is on the cards.

RaceName: Club 26 At Ascot Handicap
OwnerName: J P Morris, Mrs V A Tunnicliffe Et Al
Spotlight: C&D winner on good to firm last year; bounced back to form to win over 6f on the Rowley course here (good) in May; a lesser effort over C&D has followed but a revival isn't ruled out.

RaceName: Club 26 At Ascot Handicap
Spotlight: Took 17 attempts to get off the mark but now on a four-timer after wins at Leicester (twice) and Nottingham in recent months (all 6f on good/good to firm); only up another 2lb and merits plenty of respect in his current mood.

RaceName: Club 26 At Ascot Handicap
Spotlight: Goes well on both Newmarket courses, including a win in this race on good to firm two years ago; good third over C&D last month but behind Yes I'm Mali when only sixth back here a fortnight ago.

RaceName: Club 26 At Ascot Handicap
Spotlight: Winner over 1m on AW at Chantilly at the end of 2025 but below par on completed outing there (unseated start second occasion) at the beginning of 2026; well held over hurdles at Dieppe (2m1f, soft) in April (only start for Noel George & Amanda Zetterholm) and can only really watch dropped to sprinting for another new yard.

RaceName: Club 26 At Ascot Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up for the third time in five starts since joining John Butler when best of the rest behind the well-treated Ancient State (won next time) in 16-runner race over C&D (good) 29 days ago; same mark today.
