RaceName: Club 26 At Ascot Handicap
Spotlight: Kempton novice win (6f, AW) for Oliver Cole last October; respectable efforts in 6f/7f handicaps for new yard this year and first-time cheekpieces won't need to give him too much extra having been eased a further 2lb.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Runner-up twice over hurdles in recent weeks, latterly in refitted cheekpieces at Worcester (2m7f, good), but was well beaten in both chases last year and needs to prove he can transfer his recent good form to fences.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Not seen over fences since two disappointing attempts in spring 2024 but in good form over hurdles, notably when winning at Hexham (2m7f, good) in May; reverts to chasing on a lower mark; player if able to find some fluency.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Started off for James Moffatt with two good-ground wins in May, the latter over C&D; ran as though something was amiss here three weeks ago but could still have more improvement to come for new stable if quickly back on song; cheekpieces (worn twice in 2024) are given another whirl.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Dennis J Coppola & Mrs Dianne Sayer
Spotlight: Placed in two 2m7f Kelso chases in the autumn (good/good to soft) but absent since below-par hurdle run in February and returns with rules strike-rate standing at 0-21.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Returned from long absence with two hurdling seconds this spring (visored) and filled same position behind Kingofthegame in C&D chase three weeks ago (good; first-time blinkers); not yet exposed over fences; shortlisted.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner (including this race in 2024); good third to Halfway House over C&D in May, after a break, but pulled up after looking reluctant (not for the first time) at Hexham last month and comes with risk.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Came good with game hurdle win here (3m1f, good) in May and fended off Star Turn to follow up in C&D (good) chase three weeks ago; makes obvious appeal in current form.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden; not beaten that far when fifth of seven here (2m5f, good) three weeks ago and might be better suited by today's trip but others in this line-up have much more obvious claims.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Gained all three chase wins at this track (2m5f-3m1f) and has slipped to a dangerous mark but looked out of sorts on both starts here last month (2m5f/3m1f) and now has questions to answer.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with two poor hurdle runs for Maurice Barnes and was also well beaten when reappearing for new stable this month (first-time hood); makes chasing debut with quite a bit to prove.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to form to make all at 28-1 at York (1m, good) three weeks ago in a reapplied tongue-tie (retained); strong claims up 3lb if he can back up that latest performance.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Kicked off this campaign with two wins in a row (7f, good to soft/AW) before solid second at Newmarket (1m, good); didn't run badly in mid-division in Class 2 at Epsom (8.5f, good to soft) last time and he could be in the mix back down in grade.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time turf winner who won two in a row at Wolverhampton (8.6f/7f, AW) in March and is 1lb below his last winning mark; he ran okay on his final few starts for the Newland/Insole yard but this 8yo needs something extra on today's stable debut, following 8,500gns sale.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins last summer and he showed spark as recently as this April; he's dropped to a dangerous mark but has failed to shine on his last three outings and arrives with something to prove.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Won 1m AW maiden at Dundalk in February but not at the top of his game on final two runs for Jessica Harrington and well beaten at 28-1 on stable debut at York three weeks ago; he may be best watched.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: 11yo who needs to produce more than he's managed on his last three starts but he's 3lb lower than when a close third at Ayr (1m, good to firm) in May and has won at Ripon seven times; went close here in August 2024 when he last wore these blinkers (which replace cheekpieces); not discounted.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: In good heart this season and he returned to winning ways over C&D (good) last Monday; a 5lb rise takes this 5yo to a career-high mark but he has a solid record here and could be firmly in the shake-up once more.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Won a maiden at Leopardstown (1m, yielding) last October on her fifth start; reappeared with 2l win in the Cheshire Oaks at Chester (11.3f, good) in first-time hood and blinkers; failed to handle the track when favourite for the Oaks; change of headgear, Moore stays loyal; pick of a Ballydoyle quintet.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Group 2 winner over 6f at this venue last summer and close second to Precise in the Moyglare; well beaten in the Cheveley Park, and down the field in the Nell Gwyn on seasonal debut; sixth of 11 in the Irish 1,000 Guineas; unconvincing run in the 1m2f Pretty Polly; unappealing on first attempt at 1m4f.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: Course 7f maiden winner at two (soft to heavy); took a big step forward to win in the Listed Oaks Trial at Lingfield (good to firm) on her sixth start, and her first run beyond 1m; appeared to have a good chance of staying the Oaks trip, but faded from over two furlongs down; limitations evident at Naas.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Dual Pattern winner in busy 2025 campaign; has raced six times to much less effect since her 7f Group 2 success here last August; sole runner for Ballydoyle in the Ribblesdale, hampered and hung right; not a bad effort, but well held by Earth Shot and Johanna Walsh.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
Spotlight: 5l win at Newmarket (1m2f, good) in May on second start and then went very close against Inis Mor in a Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, good); appreciated the step up in trip when gaining a narrow win from Johanna Walsh in the Ribblesdale; highly likely to be involved in the finish.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Two 7f Newmarket wins and a fine second in France as a juvenile; fourth in the Nell Gwyn and failed to feature in the 1,000 Guineas; found her vocation when upped to 1m2f at Goodwood, getting the better of Earth Shot; enhanced her reputation with fine third behind  Diamond Necklace in the Prix de Diane; Earth Shot may have her measure at this trip.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Won at the third attempt, making all in a maiden at Leopardstown (1m2f, good) in May; took the sharp rise in class in her stride when going very close against Earth Shot in the Ribblesdale; further improvement on the cards; helps to give Joseph O'Brien a strong hand.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Debut winner on soft in a maiden at Gowran in March, relegating stablemate Johanna Walsh to third; coped well with good ground and a furlong further when landing a Group 3 at Naas; now takes on her Oaks-winning stable's Thundering On and the much-improved Johanna Walsh.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Unbeaten filly; won a Naas maiden at 25-1 on debut (1m, good) and confirmed ability when showing a good turn of foot in a slowly-run Gowran conditions race (9.5f good to yielding); deserves to take her chance after a Group 3 win over this trip at Cork.

RaceName: Juddmonte Irish Oaks (Group 1) (Fillies)
OwnerName: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith
Spotlight: Third in Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster last September and readily made all in Group 3 here in October; fourth of five behind Amelia Earhart at Chester on reappearance, reversed form with her disappointing stablemate when third at Epsom, weakened late, beaten almost 10l; first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Betway Boosts Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Made it 3-5 over fences when scoring at Bangor (3m, good) in May and he went very close behind his main market rival at Fontwell four weeks later; up another 2lb but he still looks on a workable mark and is open to more progress as a chaser; respected.

RaceName: Betway Boosts Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Career has had its ups and downs but he won two chases (3m1f and 3m6f) in March 2025 and he's been placed at Stratford (second of five) and Worcester (third of 11) in his last two runs; remains well treated off 10lb lower than his last success and he's in the mix back up in trip.

RaceName: Betway Boosts Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Readers & Wiggy, The 8 Amigos & J Burns
Spotlight: Six of his nine wins have come at Market Rasen including over C&D; only 1lb higher than for his win at Wetherby in March but he's been vulnerable in three runs since and needs to rediscover his spark.

RaceName: Betway Boosts Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 6yo who made it 4-10 over fences when completing a double with his late thrust at Hexham (3m, good) last month; up 3lb but he's been as good as ever in his last two starts and he's strong stayer as this trip; key player.

RaceName: Betway Boosts Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Appreciated the step back up to 3m when winning at Warwick (good) in May but he was a well-held fourth when favourite at Worcester (2m7f) last month; has bit to prove again after that and this is competitive.

RaceName: Betway Boosts Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won over hurdles at Hexham (2m4f, good) and Musselburgh (3m, soft) last season; only 1lb higher than for his last success but he's struggled in his last three runs and has something to prove on chase debut.

RaceName: Betway Boosts Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Just 1-11 in British points but she's shown some ability in novice/maiden hurdles this year; now makes her handicap/chase debut and she needs checking in market on this big step back up in trip.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Xvii & M Parker
Spotlight: Incurs 4lb penalty for a Windsor success (6f, good to firm; made all) that hasn't worked out well but brings upside, being a once-raced colt who is in good hands and may progress.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Naval Crown colt who showed promise with close fifth of 11 at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) having attracted support; possibilities with progress on the cards.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 100,000gns yearling; by Mehmas and first foal of Listed-placed 5f/6f 2yo winner (RPR 94), from family of top miler Attraction; stablemate of once-raced Can't Stop; market instructive.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 150,000euros foal, 90,000gns yearling, 140,000gns (brreeze-up) 2yo; Starspangledbanner half-brother to four winners, notably Harrow (Group-placed 6.5f-7.3f 2yo; RPR 102); dam 5f AW winner (67); interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Finished second of four in Yarmouth maiden (6f, good to firm) won by fellow newcomer who was odds-on and looks a useful prospect; respected assuming he builds on that promise.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 55,000gns yearling; Mattmu half-brother to two winners, notably Anaisa (5f/6f 2yo including Listed; RPR 80); dam 1m1f winner (78); the stable second string on jockey arrangements.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 27,000euros foal, 55,000euros yearling; Naval Crown half-brother to three winners, notably Romanovich (7f-8.6f; RPR 67); dam maiden (75); yard first string on jockey arrangements; betting may give further guidance.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Brings the worst form, having finished last of seven in 6f contest on the turf course at Lingfield; gelded since; perhaps best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 11,000gns foal, 60,000gns yearling; Harry Angel half-brother to six winners. notably Hoodwinker (1m-1m2f inc AW 2yo; RPR 92); dam unraced; notable connections; heed the market signals.

RaceName: Back The Super Boost At BetVictor EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 120,000euros yearling, 370,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; by Too Darn Hot; sibling to winners in Poland and Sweden; dam unraced, out of Oaks/King George winner Taghrooda; looks the pick of the debutants.

RaceName: National Stud 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses With No More Than One Win)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing & Harry Kassap Llc
Spotlight: Has found his level since handicapping over 1m, finishing close up on Kempton AW in May before showing the right spirit to hold off his rivals at Ripon (good); less convincing with his finishing effort at Windsor since but dam's side of pedigree suggests this trip could suit.

RaceName: National Stud 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses With No More Than One Win)
Spotlight: He's been up against some good rivals in his qualifying runs but has shown signs of ability and there's every chance he'll give a good show now switched to this new level.

RaceName: National Stud 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses With No More Than One Win)
OwnerName: Dr Jamal Ahmadzadeh & Mrs D Swinburn
Spotlight: Looked likely to pick up a 1m2f handicap on the form of his first attempt in one, when runner-up at Doncaster in April, but that hasn't happened and he just plugged on at one pace when seventh of 11 at Newbury recently; down in grade but others higher up the list.

RaceName: National Stud 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses With No More Than One Win)
Spotlight: Step forward when keeping on at one pace in first handicap, on Lingfield AW (1m) in January; off the track and gelded after but he came home with a wet sail when a close third of six at Salisbury (1m, good to firm) recently; looks well worth a go at 1m2f; respected.

RaceName: National Stud 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses With No More Than One Win)
Spotlight: Showed just modest form as a juvenile and she was beaten a fair way out on comeback in her first handicap, on Wolverhampton AW in first-time cheekpieces; upped to 1m2f with plenty to find.

RaceName: National Stud 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses With No More Than One Win)
Spotlight: Out of unraced half-sister to Group 1 winners at 1m2f in Hong Kong; made no impact in four 2yo starts at about 7f but he did show some promise behind highly talented rivals (the winner just lost out in a recent Group 1 in France) on his Beverley debut and sharp AW tests wouldn't have suited after; everything points to improvement for the new trip now handicapping.

RaceName: Shorefield Supplies Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Both wins over fences came over C&D in 2024; well handicapped on his old form but he's generally struggled in the last 12 months and was last of seven over C&D (good) when last seen in May; plenty to prove and all of his wins have been on soft or heavy ground.

RaceName: Shorefield Supplies Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Defied this mark over C&D (good) last August but he's been out of sorts in his last three runs and latest was at Cartmel three weeks ago; has something to prove and he needs to get back on track; blinkers are removed and cheekpieces return.

RaceName: Shorefield Supplies Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 11-race maiden who has a patchy profile and made it 0-5 over fences when pulled up at Market Rasen (2m6f, good) in May; comes with risks attached but he may have had stamina issues last time and he looks interesting on his placed effort at Hereford (2m, good) in March; tongue-tie and cheekpieces are removed and visor returns.

RaceName: Shorefield Supplies Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Record of 5-18 over fences but all of those wins have come at Sedgefield (2m/2m4f); only 2lb higher than for his last win in April but he's not gone on from that and was a well-held fifth of seven over C&D (good; 16-1) last time; others preferred.

RaceName: Shorefield Supplies Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Triple hurdle winner who is 0-8 over fences but he's on a dangerous mark and wasn't beaten far when third at Market Rasen (2m2f, good) on penultimate run; has possibilities if he can get back near that level and yard is in good form; wore blinkers last time but regular cheekpieces now return.

RaceName: Shorefield Supplies Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won off 16lb higher at Worcester (2m4f, good) in August 2024 but he's lost his way and has failed to complete in his last three runs; has lots to prove and he needs a major revival on this big drop back in trip.

RaceName: Shorefield Supplies Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Overall record of 0-12 but she's unexposed over fences and went clear some way out before fading into a 9l fourth here (2m5f, good) three weeks ago; continues to edge down the weights and she could make a bold bid on this drop back in trip; hood removed.

RaceName: Shorefield Supplies Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Four-time chase winner who bounced back with a close second at Uttoxeter (2m, good) 11 days ago; 3lb higher here but he remains well treated on old form and all of his wins have been under Brian Hughes; key player.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well for a long way in front before fading into fourth when upped to 1m at Redcar (good) four weeks ago; it's possible she'd benefit from the return to 7f but is not ruled out today.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: It's still early days and there's potential in his pedigree; market check advised but he's finished well beaten in both handicaps and needs to be an entirely different proposition today.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Won two in a row this spring (1m, firm/good) and has continued in good heart, finishing second of three at Bath (1m, firm) then third of nine at Nottingham (1m2f, good to firm); shaped last time as though he may benefit from the return to 1m and he could be thereabouts.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Improved upon her previous form when dropped back in trip at Leicester (1m, good to firm) a fortnight ago, finishing third; this attractively bred filly could have more to offer and is a possible contender.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Triple-figure odds on all three starts; down the field on first two outings and same story at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) last month after a break, but there were signs of ability that day; this half-sister to Group 3 winner Making Dreams goes handicapping off a modest mark and it will be no surprise if she plays a leading role, especially if strong in the betting.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
OwnerName: D Bishop, S Bland & T Coyle
Spotlight: Didn't run badly at Beverley (8.4f, good to firm) a fortnight ago when fifth of seven in first-time cheekpieces (retained); however, he probably needs more help from the handicapper before he posts his first win.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Won over C&D (good) in May in first-time cheekpieces (retained since); pulled hard when 7l third at Leicester (1m, good) next time and down the field at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) most recently, but the return to Ripon could trigger a revival.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Ran okay on her first two starts this season and has dropped down the weights but she's struggled on her last three runs; major turnaround needed in a first-time hood (replacing the cheekpieces adopted last time).

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Built on some encouraging spring form to get off the mark at the 12th attempt in a C&D handicap (good) last month, beating a runner-up who won his next two; unplaced favourite in Thirsk classified (1m, good to firm) on Thursday but was caught wide; might be worth another chance.

RaceName: Hill Of Allen Stud Handicap
OwnerName: David Spratt & Sean Jones & Mrs Lynne Lyons
Spotlight: Failed to win at two, but got off the mark on second start of this season in 1m2f (yielding) maiden at Navan; never landed a blow on handicap debut in the King George V handicap at Royal Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) last time; capable of better, but has to prove he handles quick ground.

RaceName: Hill Of Allen Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Handicap debutante; shed the maiden tag at the third attempt over 1m4f at Fairyhouse (good) 17 days ago; opening mark doesn't look overly generous and it's debatable whether this drop back in trip will suit, but unexposed and can't be discounted.

RaceName: Hill Of Allen Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Cork maiden winner in May (1m2f, soft); beaten off 78 in handicap on her previous start (also at Cork) over 1m4f (gd-yld) and is 5lb higher now; remote third in 1m4f (soft) Listed race at Leopardstown on latest; drop back in trip a plus and probably capable of better.

RaceName: Hill Of Allen Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Handicap debutante; placed in all three maiden starts at 1m-10.5f on varying ground; found just one too good at Fairyhouse on latest (1m2f, good) where she had Tanzanite Diamond 5.5l behind; bred to be better than her opening mark of 78 and interesting runner for yard that won this last year and in 2021.

RaceName: Hill Of Allen Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Unplaced in all three maidens but has shown ability; stayed on well over 1m at Leopardstown in May; well behind Noble Honour and Tanzanite Diamond at Fairyhouse upped to 1m2f (good) on latest; gelded since and blinkers go on for handicap debut; could improve, but others have stronger form claims.

RaceName: Hill Of Allen Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Fairly modest form in four maidens; behind Noble Honour at Fairyhouse on penultimate over 1m2f (good); beaten 13l when fourth of 17 over 1m4f (good) back there on latest; tongue tie and cheekpieces go on for handicap debut; probably best watched.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hard-fought win back over hurdles at Hexham (2m) before a 10l defeat returned to fences; no great significance in the return of cheekpieces (blinkered last two races) but entitled to have some say.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Ursa Major Ltd & Hicky Parmar Racing
Spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner who was second in a Cheltenham handicap last October on final jumps run; 2lb lower now and he was a recent 2m winner at Newmarket; should go well.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: All of his seven wins have been at either Hexham or here, so merits attention after finishing a close third at Uttoxeter a month ago; 2lb below last winning mark.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 5lb higher than for his runaway win at Hereford (2m; handicap debut); fell early next time and then only fourth of the five runners at Worcester; probably needs his best yet here but it's still early days.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Had a lengthy break after her breakthrough win but better than ever a month ago when breezing home off 9lb lower over C&D; something seems to have clicked.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Inconsistent 9yo who kept on for third at Uttoxeter (2m4f) without threatening; others preferred.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Fontwell maiden winner last April; some good runs since in cheekpieces and the visor tried last time is soon dispensed with; could feature off what should be a good mark.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Bumper winner who is 0-9 over hurdles but has been bang there in his last two races, over C&D and 2m7f at Worcester; raised 3lb but he enters the reckoning.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has returned from a break in good form, winning back-to-back Class 5 handicaps at about 2m4f on good ground (the first here); remains on a fair mark but this is a better race and a competitive one.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Runner-up in a bumper for former Irish yard; three heavy defeats in four runs for current one and was tailed off on handicap debut; has since had a wind operation and now goes in cheekpieces.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has run well in all four handicaps; may need another jolt of improvement to defy current mark but now tries cheekpieces.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Joined Fergal O'Brien after finishing well beaten in a selling handicap; dropped to a good mark on best form and prevailed in a tight finish at Worcester 17 days ago (2m); respected off 4lb higher.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Hindered by unfavourable draw (fared best in her group) at Royal Ascot last time; progressive on AW otherwise, posting form figures of 3111, and may still have more to offer; remains of interest, assuming she can prove as effective on turf.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Absent since running creditably in valuable race at Deauville (6.5f) last August; won easily at Doncaster (7f) the time before; still unexposed and looks interesting on reappearance, especially if the market speaks positively.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Not particularly solid on her 1m form this term but wasn't entirely disgraced at Royal Ascot on penultimate start; appears likely to benefit from this drop back in trip/class and return to the scene of one of her 2yo wins; could go well.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Effective over 6f as a 2yo (won at Newbury) but the return to that distance backfired last time; good second in a couple of 7f contests otherwise this season and remains less exposed over this trip; possibilities.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: HM The King & HM The Queen
Spotlight: Has a record of 0-5 since debut success (6f) but she ran encouragingly upped in trip and fitted with a hood at Newmarket (good to firm) most recently; may build on that effort kept to 7f in the retained headgear; now 5lb lower than her opening mark; respected.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: Justified favouritism in another Class 5 event last week, taking Catterick record to 3-3 and strike-rate since handicapping to 4-6; Newmarket win shows she's versatile as regards track; upped in class but receives weight all round and possibly hasn't stopped improving yet.

RaceName: Lodge At The National Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Best form at 1m on AW, winning three times on Newcastle Tapeta, but he wasn't doing so well when last seen in the spring and, although he has turf form at 1m, it's offputting he's seen so rarely on the surface.

RaceName: Lodge At The National Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins at 1m2f in 2024 but he has run well over this C&D and, after a slow start to the year, he showed more when a close third of eight at Ascot (1m, good to firm) last week; on the premises again.

RaceName: Lodge At The National Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Impressive winner of two of his three AW handicaps as 2yo, at 7f and 1m, and his reappearance fifth of nine on the Rowley course (1m, good to firm) in May hinted at better to come; never reached a challenging position over C&D in June but has less to do this time; has to be considered.

RaceName: Lodge At The National Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Turf wins at 7f and latest success was over 6f on AW in April; has rarely tackled 1m and she was highly tried over this trip in the Kensington Palace at Royal Ascot in June but she did look to stay an extended 1m at Nottingham in 2025 for previous yard; has possibilities.

RaceName: Lodge At The National Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Most of his handicaps on AW, winning three at 7f and 1m, but the return to turf is working quite well of late, gaining a first win on the surface at Goodwood (1m, good to soft) in June then checked in quite a promising run at Sandown (1m, good to firm) since; can be competitive off the same mark.

RaceName: Lodge At The National Stud Handicap
Spotlight: A stout stayer over 1m and Salisbury's stiff test suited him in May (good) where he overcame trouble in running to lead near the line; has had excuses since but he has a good run in him if this is truly run.

RaceName: Lodge At The National Stud Handicap
Spotlight: Had some good 2yo form, as when winning both starts at about 7f, and this campaign has a somewhat luckless look to it so far, albeit slow starts aren't helping; his one start over 1m, in a valuable Carlisle handicap in May, was inconclusive regarding stamina but he remains of interest.

RaceName: Charnley's Home & Garden Centre Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at around 3m during the opening quarter of 2025 but following a year off he's refused to race on two occasions and was pulled up here (3m1f, good) last time.

RaceName: Charnley's Home & Garden Centre Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Nine-time chase winner including twice at Cartmel; didn't fire at Worcester in May but that was after a close second at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) and he's a player if he can recapture that form; not ruled out for yard that won this last year.

RaceName: Charnley's Home & Garden Centre Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Major improver who made it 5-7 over fences when completing a five-timer over C&D (good) three weeks ago; up another 5lb but he's on a roll and this tough 7yo could continue on the upgrade; big player.

RaceName: Charnley's Home & Garden Centre Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won twice over hurdles and once over fences for Dan Skelton; wasn't beaten far when third of three on stable debut here (2m1f, good) in May but he was well held in another small-field event over C&D last time; still lightly raced over fences but he needs to step up again after that last run; cheekpieces reapplied.

RaceName: Charnley's Home & Garden Centre Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: All five wins over fences have been at this track and latest was over C&D (good) in May; didn't shine here last time but that was a Class 2 handicap and he still looks on a workable mark; respected on this big drop back in grade.

RaceName: Charnley's Home & Garden Centre Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has won four chases and latest was at Newcastle in October (2m4f, good); well handicapped on that form but he's been disappointing since and was pulled up at Perth two weeks ago.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Course winner who is well handicapped on last season's form and is one to watch in the betting; however, he finished down the field on his three starts this spring and needs to turn things around.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: 13-race maiden who has been expensive to follow and underwhelming on his last two starts; however, his season began with two positive performances in the spring and he's in the picture if rediscovering that form.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Runner-up on his first three starts (6f/5f, AW), the latest at Southwell in May, but he's struggled to get competitive on turf in his four handicaps; needs to raise his game.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Third on handicap/seasonal debut at Nottingham (5f, good) in May and bounced back from lesser run to finish second at Redcar (6f, good) four weeks ago; got loose and withdrawn at Beverley three days later (5-2 at the time); subsequently sold for 4,200gns; respected on stable debut.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: He's regressed and is winless since scoring over C&D in July 2024; only fourth of six on stable debut at Chepstow (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago but he was the 7-4 favourite and got upset in the stalls, and may have needed that run; interesting to see if he's again strong in the betting.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: 13-race maiden who may be better at 5f but he's run some good races this year, including beaten just a nose at Beverley (5f, good to firm) two starts ago, and he might not be far away.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Just one win from his 22 starts and merely mid-division at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) last time in first-time blinkers (retained); however, he's twice finished runner-up this season and is not ruled out.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
OwnerName: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing
Spotlight: Four-time course winner whose mark continues to fall and he hasn't been devoid of spark this season; however, this 9yo needs to better what he's shown on his last three starts (the first of them over C&D).

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Her 22 starts have yielded just one win but she was a close second of 18 at 25-1 at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) two starts ago and backed that up when runner-up to at Catterick (7f, good to firm) last Wednesday, albeit no match for the thriving winner; firmly in calculations given recent showings.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden who wasn't quite at his best when fourth of nine over C&D (good) last week but was in good form previously, coming second at Redcar (7f, good) in May and at Catterick (6f, good to firm) last month; chance.

RaceName: Aldwark Manor Estate Luxury Yorkshire Hotel 'Confined' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: In good form in the main this year, twice running well at Pontefract (5f/6f, good) last month before respectable fourth of seven at Hamilton (6f, good to firm) on Thursday; possible player.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Summer Festival Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Took advantage of a declining mark to register her first AW/handicap win, at Southwell (5f) last month; hung right throughout when fourth of five at Hamilton (5f, good to firm) since; should not be far away if on her best behaviour.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Summer Festival Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Scored off a career-low mark to land his eighth success, at Epsom (6f, good to firm) nine days ago; back up 2lb but has recorded back-to-back wins previously so cannot be discounted.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Summer Festival Apprentice Handicap

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs C Fruhbeck and Denford Stud
Spotlight: Made virtually all to get off the mark in a Kempton novice (7f) in February; made an encouraging handicap debut when beaten less than 2l at Newbury (1m2f, good to firm) 16 days ago; she should not be far away if that runs brings her on.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice in Britain for current yard since winning twice in France; however, beaten 21l at Leicester (1m2f, good) last month following five-month layoff and lacks the scope of his rivals.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Has gained all of his three wins on the AW but is knocking on the door for his first turf win; placed on his last two runs, latest when runner-up at Leicester (1m2f, good) last month; claims.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 18th attempt over C&D (good to firm) two weeks ago off 3lb lower on his second run for this yard, with Jake Dickson aboard him; needs to come on again in this higher grade.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice on AW for Marco Botti; scored over hurdles last September; all but refused to race when trailing in last of four at Ffos Las (1m4f, good) on Monday; one to have serious reservations about at present.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Showed much improved form when stepping up in trip on his handicap debut to be runner-up at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; should step on from that and holds strong claims.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Dual Flat and four-time hurdle winner who has finished runner-up at Lingfield (2m, AW/good) in his two Flat runs this year; still looks feasibly treated in this sphere and he ran respectably back over jumps at Uttoxeter on Wednesday; respected.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Big improver over hurdles and he made it 5-8 in that sphere when completing a hat-trick at Perth (2m, good) two weeks ago; looks interesting off dangerous mark on the Flat and he won over 1m7f in France as a 3yo.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: 0-6 but she ran her best race so far when close second in a Sandown handicap (1m6f, good) two weeks ago; has shaped as though this step up to 2m should be fine and she's strongly respected off only 1lb higher than last time.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Dam is related to St Leger winner Hurricane Lane and Group-class stayer Sweet William; consistent form in her first five starts but she raised her game upped to 1m4f when runner-up in an Epsom handicap (good) 17 days ago; nudged up 2lb but she kept on well and is open to more progress now switched to a staying trip; needs a close look.

NYRA Bets Haskell Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
OwnerName: Bran Jam Stable & David W Clark
Spotlight: Useful form in Listed and black type events but no form close to the level needed to defeat these.

RaceName: NYRA Bets Haskell Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
Spotlight: Very impressive in Grade 1 Blue Grass Stakes at Keeneland (1m1f; won by 11l) in May but disappointing favourite for Grade 1 Kentucky Derby (11th of 18, never looking likely to take hand in finish); returned to best to win Grade 3 Matt Winn Stakes at Churchill Downs in June by 2l; seems to need daylight tracking leaders and should get that kind of scenario here.

RaceName: NYRA Bets Haskell Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
OwnerName: Cosmo Stables And Delta Squad Racing, Llc
Spotlight: Impressive winner of Listed Pegasus Stakes here (8.5f), the acknowledged local prep for this; that form still leaves him with a lot to find.

RaceName: NYRA Bets Haskell Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
OwnerName: OGMA Investments LLC, JR Ranch & High Step Racing LLC
Spotlight: Very smart form in four starts, winning Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby (8.5f) in March but better effort still when beaten nose by Commandment in Grade 1 Florida Derby (1m1f) later same month; missed Kentucky Derby (and training time since) with skin infection but could still take a hand after the layoff.

RaceName: NYRA Bets Haskell Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
OwnerName: St Elias Stable, William H Lawrence & Glassman Racing
Spotlight: Successful on first two starts, both at Aqueduct, in maiden last December and Grade 3 Gotham Stakes (1m; by 1l) in April; ran best race yet when second to Napoleon Solo in Grade 1 Preakness Stakes at Laurel (9.5f) in May, closing to just over 1l behind winner; open to more improvement and strong claims.

RaceName: NYRA Bets Haskell Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
Spotlight: Strong-running horse with a ton of early pace; career-best effort to beat Iron Honor by just over 1l in Preakness Stakes at Laurel (9.5f; second Grade 1 win) in May; travelled really strongly to take command there and value for more than winning margin (had suffered foot problems and may have been short of work); could still be open to more improvement and likely the one to beat.

RaceName: NYRA Bets Haskell Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
OwnerName: Ashley Durr, Anthony Tate & Front Page Equestrian LLC
Spotlight: Late-running horse capable of smart efforts but not necessarily wins of same class; in frame in Grade 1 company in May in Kentucky Derby and Preakness Stakes; another belated effort when 1l second to Chip Honcho in Grade 3 Ohio Derby at Thistledown (1m1f) last month; strong chance of a minor place.
